title: Truancy and Tardiness
wikitext:
[]: Truancy and Tardiness             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Wed Aug 13, 2008 1:32 pm I don't think it's mentioned very much, but not surprisingly, many of the characters miss class or are late to things. I'm pretty sure it's a pretty long list. So what are all the cases of tardiness/truancy you can think of?

[]:             Quote Post by swamp147 » Wed Aug 13, 2008 1:40 pm Hmm, off the top of my head: - Toji missing several classes when he was selected as Eva pilot, then missing all his classes when he ended in the hospital after Bardiel. - Shinji's skipping school in the beginning of the series (like episode 4) - Rei missing classes in the beginning from injuries - might not count, but I'll include it anywyas; all 3 eva pilots missing the class trip in case an angel comes - Shinji's injuries/shocks; like the first time he fought Ramiel and got owned. - Asuka's depression; plays video games instead of going to class - random synch tests? - random angels? Wasn't too specific, but I think this covers a lot of the truancy and tardiness.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Wed Aug 13, 2008 1:41 pm Well, you can't count angel attacks due to the whole city shutting down. Rei is late all the time due to injury, so that would obviously be a medical excuse. But Shinji runs away for a while, so he would have been the one punished, but Nerv more than likely fixed it. I don't know if Asuka was ever absent or tardy, so that's all I got for now.

[]:             Quote Post by swamp147 » Wed Aug 13, 2008 1:47 pm Oh wait, truancy is different from missing class for a medical or other legitimate reason. Crapz0rZ. Well, I'll leave my post as it is. In this case though, scratch most of what I mentioned in my first post here.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Aug 13, 2008 3:55 pm Rei is always absent when she's doing those private tests with Gendo. And what is the point of this thread?

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Thu Aug 14, 2008 11:55 pm ^ The point is that people who are truants or are late all the time often times have problems. Why do you always rip on my threads?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Aug 15, 2008 12:35 am The only people focused on in Eva are the ones with problems. It's never said if anybody else is truant or often late so one cannot make the assumption that because they have problems they're truant or often late because of it. Also. Once again, this is Eva. We don't need to know if they're truant or often late to know if they have problems. We can tell they have problems just by watching the show. And I rip on your threads because they are often pointless, and have little to contribute to anything.

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Fri Aug 15, 2008 12:45 am Stuff that's not all that serious belongs in 'chit chat' don't it? If it's a real serious debate, then it goes in 'DISCUSSION'. Lighten up, jackass. Okay, so there's a defined question here, how's: how does truancy/tardiness relate to the psychological problems of the characters in Eva? Specific cases in the show and proven psychological fact, much appreciated. There, happy?

[]:             Quote Post by swamp147 » Fri Aug 15, 2008 5:02 am Umm Asuka playing video games instead of going to school because she feels like her life is worthless because the only thing that has kept herself satisfied - being an exceptional Eva pilot - is now no more?

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Fri Aug 15, 2008 5:24 am How about ep8 when they all went on the aircraft carrier for no reason other than shits and giggles.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Fri Aug 15, 2008 10:55 am ^ that was probably on the weekend. Maybe on a Saturday?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Aug 15, 2008 11:03 am Japan treats truancy differently to Anglosphere -- think, for example, of Jun in Rozen Maiden .

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer_2k4 » Fri Aug 15, 2008 11:41 am Iron Rooster wrote: The point is that people who are truants or are late all the time often times have problems.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Fri Aug 15, 2008 11:49 am Hikari's lines imply Asuka has been playing all day, at the expense of everything else. Including school.

[]:             Quote Post by swamp147 » Fri Aug 15, 2008 3:05 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Japan treats truancy differently to Anglosphere -- think, for example, of Jun in Rozen Maiden .

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Aug 15, 2008 3:12 pm Schooling is a much more voluntary activity in Japan -- there's no requirement to attend or be equivalently home-schooled. Instead there is more the societal pressure to get ahead in the rat race.

[]:             Quote Post by swamp147 » Fri Aug 15, 2008 3:22 pm Ah, thanks, that's pretty cool actually. It could be better to have students study because they want to become successful, not because "omg you must go to school NOW or suffer consequences!!" I think I'd like that system better than the one in the US.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Aug 15, 2008 6:15 pm Action_Bastard wrote: ^ that was probably on the weekend. Maybe on a Saturday?

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Fri Aug 15, 2008 6:16 pm Oh, ok, thanks. "^ that was probably on the weekend. Maybe on a Sunday?"

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Fri Aug 15, 2008 11:11 pm In a sense, isn't going to school/work to be a success a way of avoiding the consequence of being a failure? Devil's advocate, he he. Now, when talking about these matters in Eva, there are some things that come to mind for me. I do remember in the show, Misato was late to the command center during an angel attack (leliel?). I do not understand why someone like her would let herself be late. With Shinji, I could understand not going to school; he does what he's told until something is too much for him. Asuka, I don't know. I found it odd she did not skip before her mind fell apart since she did not really care for her grades in a Japanese school in the first place. Maybe she was trying to maintain an image as a success to other people who don't know?

I'm pretty sure it's a pretty long list. So what are all the cases of tardiness/truancy you can think of?

[]:             Quote Post by swamp147 » Wed Aug 13, 2
